trim_reasons: Spread of -13.5 is the largest blowout risk on the slate — even with BLOWOUT penalty applied, SGA is the primary star on the favored team and faces genuine Q4 rest/garbage-time removal risk in a potential rout. The VOLATILE tag means his distribution is wide, and a blowout scenario can compress his counting stats well below his 29.0 raw average even at T20.

BOS is favored by -12.5 with BLOWOUT_RISK, and Brown's FG_MARGIN_THIN of 4% (near-zero cushion) means even modest defensive resistance or a blowout-induced minutes reduction could push him below T15. The mandatory step-down from T20 was already required by the thin margin, meaning this pick lives on a structurally fragile floor.

The PHI 3PM suppressor is extreme (-45.6pp per scout), and while quant override was applied, historical audit lessons confirm 3PM props are the highest-variance prop type — iron_floor is absent here. Blowout risk (-12.5) can further compress late-game three-point attempt volume if BOS pulls starters early.

VOLATILE tag with iron_floor=false at T15 and a -12.5 blowout game creates a structurally soft floor — Tatum is the primary star on the favored team most likely to be rested in Q4 of a decisive game, and his raw_avg of 22.4 is lower than Brown's, leaving less cushion against blowout-induced minute compression.

The pick_reasoning explicitly flags a calibration note to AVOID 3PM (-11pp drop in playoffs) yet the pick is included at 80% confidence — this internal tension is the sharpest bear case. The PHI suppressor is extreme (-45.6pp) and iron_floor is absent; a blowout could reduce Tatum's three-point attempts in Q4 precisely when he needs them to clear T2.

Pritchard's min_floor of 23.4 with avg_minutes of 28.1 means a blowout-induced early bench in a -12.5 game could easily push him below the minutes needed to attempt enough threes for T2; the PHI 3PM suppressor (40% hit rate, -45.6pp per scout) represents genuine structural drag even with the quant override.

Min_floor of 21.6 on a secondary scorer in a -13.5 blowout game is structurally fragile — Holmgren is the most likely OKC rotation player to see compressed Q4 minutes in a rout, and a single low-minutes game (entirely within normal variance) is sufficient to produce a miss at T10 with iron_floor only barely providing a floor.

VOLATILE REB pick with iron_floor=false, post_miss_hr of only 83% (lift barely above 1.0), and a min_floor of just 21.6 minutes in a -13.5 blowout game — blowout-induced minute reduction directly compresses rebounding opportunities for a big man. The combination of VOLATILE tag, no iron_floor, and extreme blowout risk on the favored side creates a structurally fragile REB floor that the 78% confidence does not adequately capture.

Williams has the lowest min_floor on the slate at 19.4 minutes, and at 70% confidence with VOLATILE tag and a post_miss_hr of only 67% (lift=0.92, below 1.0 — no bounce-back effect), this pick sits at minimum confidence with structurally compressed minute protection in a -13.5 blowout game.

VOLATILE tag with iron_floor=false and post_miss_hr of only 71% (lift=0.80 — the only sub-1.0 bounce-back lift on the slate) means Wembanyama has a confirmed pattern of sequential misses, not a bounce-back effect. At T15 in a -10.5 blowout game with a -opp defense that is trending tougher over L5, the floor is genuinely fragile.
